code: @Manual{,   title = {gp3bayes: Contract-First Bayesian Workflows for Hierarchical Behavioural Data},   author = {Stefanos Balaskas},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.0.0.9000},   url = {https://stefanosbalaskas.github.io/gp3bayes/}, }

title: gp3bayes
text: gp3bayes independent R package development transparent, contract-first Bayesian workflows repeated-measures hierarchical behavioural data.

title: gp3bayes
text: package designed around: explicit data model contracts; model-readiness audits; deterministic simulation recorded generating parameters; inspectable prior specifications; prior posterior predictive checks; convergence sampling diagnostics; prior-sensitivity assessment; parameter-recovery workflows; conservative reproducible reporting. initial development scope restricted : hierarchical Bernoulli-logit models binary trial-level outcomes; hierarchical lognormal models strictly positive uncensored durations. core package require Gazepoint hardware, Gazepoint exports, gp3tools, proprietary software, private data, Bayesian backend.

title: gp3bayes
text: create_model_contract() records approved methodological specification neutral column mappings one initial model family. Creating contract validate data, fit model, establish model adequacy.
code: binary_contract <- create_model_contract(   family = "binary",   outcome_col = "selected",   participant_col = "participant_id",   item_col = "stimulus_id",   trial_col = "trial_id",   condition_col = "condition" )  binary_contract ## <gp3bayes_model_contract> ##   Family: binary ##   Likelihood: Bernoulli ##   Link: logit ##   Outcome: selected ##   Participant: participant_id ##   Item: stimulus_id ##   Condition: condition ##   Random slope requested: FALSE ##   Fitting performed: FALSE

title: gp3bayes
text: audit_model_readiness() evaluates observable data requirements formula construction model fitting. Failures block progression, whereas warnings identify structures requiring review.
code: binary_data <- data.frame(   participant_id = rep(c("p1", "p2"), each = 4),   stimulus_id = rep(paste0("s", 1:4), times = 2),   trial_id = rep(1:4, times = 2),   condition = rep(c("control", "treatment"), times = 4),   selected = c(0, 1, 0, 1, 1, 0, 1, 0) )  readiness_audit <- audit_model_readiness(   binary_data,   binary_contract )  readiness_audit ## <gp3bayes_readiness_audit> ##   Family: binary ##   Rows: 8 ##   Status: ready ##   Ready: TRUE ##   Checks: 18 passed, 0 warnings, 0 failures

title: gp3bayes
text: gp3bayes currently development version 0.0.0.9000. public model-fitting API available yet. current development stage establishes standalone package structure, approved methodological scope, data-contract principles, validation infrastructure.

title: gp3bayes
text: Behavioural, gaze, pupil, physiological measurements directly reveal emotion, stress, cognition, comprehension, personality, diagnosis, deception, intention, latent psychological states. Associations must described causal effects unless study design target estimand justify causal interpretation.

title: gp3bayes
text: gp3bayes released MIT License.

what: Audit Data Readiness for an Approved Model Contract — audit_model_readiness
title: Audit Data Readiness for an Approved Model Contract — audit_model_readiness
text: Audits whether data frame satisfies observable data requirements existing model contract created create_model_contract(). audit backend-independent construct formula, define executable priors, fit model, establish model adequacy.

title: Audit Data Readiness for an Approved Model Contract — audit_model_readiness
Audit Data Readiness for an Approved Model Contract — audit_model_readiness
text: data data frame containing declared outcome, grouping identifiers, predictors, optional design columns. contract gp3bayes_model_contract created create_model_contract().

title: Audit Data Readiness for an Approved Model Contract — audit_model_readiness
text: object class gp3bayes_readiness_audit. object records: whether data ready proceed later model-building gate; pass, warning, failure counts; one structured row per readiness check; declared observed column summaries; audited model contract. input data retained returned object.

title: Audit Data Readiness for an Approved Model Contract — audit_model_readiness
text: readiness audit evaluates observable data properties . Passing audit establish convergence, model adequacy, predictive validity, causal identification, substantive validity. Failures block progression later model-building gate. Warnings identify weak unusual structures require review automatically block progression. Binary outcomes must logical numeric values encoded exclusively zero one, classes observed. Duration outcomes must numeric, finite, strictly positive, uncensored, variable.

title: Audit Data Readiness for an Approved Model Contract — audit_model_readiness
text: Readiness checks determine whether model scientifically justified. Behavioural measurements must interpreted direct measures latent psychological protected attributes.

title: Audit Data Readiness for an Approved Model Contract — audit_model_readiness
code: binary_data <- data.frame(   participant_id = rep(c("p1", "p2"), each = 4),   trial_id = rep(1:4, times = 2),   condition = rep(c("control", "treatment"), times = 4),   selected = c(0, 1, 0, 1, 1, 0, 1, 0) )  binary_contract <- create_model_contract(   family = "binary",   outcome_col = "selected",   participant_col = "participant_id",   trial_col = "trial_id",   condition_col = "condition" )  audit_model_readiness(binary_data, binary_contract) #> <gp3bayes_readiness_audit> #>   Family: binary #>   Rows: 8 #>   Status: ready #>   Ready: TRUE #>   Checks: 17 passed, 0 warnings, 0 failures

what: Create an Approved Bayesian Model Contract — create_model_contract
title: Create an Approved Bayesian Model Contract — create_model_contract
text: Creates inspectable model-contract object one two model families approved initial gp3bayes development scope. function records neutral data-column mappings preserving approved likelihood, link, estimands, assumptions, diagnostics, sensitivity requirements, interpretation boundaries.

title: Create an Approved Bayesian Model Contract — create_model_contract
code: create_model_contract(   family,   outcome_col,   participant_col,   item_col = NULL,   trial_col = NULL,   condition_col = NULL,   time_col = NULL,   predictors = character(),   interaction = NULL,   random_slope = FALSE,   outcome_unit = NULL,   notes = character() )

title: Create an Approved Bayesian Model Contract — create_model_contract
text: family Character scalar identifying approved model family. Supported values "binary" "duration". outcome_col Character scalar naming outcome column. participant_col Character scalar naming participant identifier column. item_col Optional character scalar naming item stimulus identifier column. trial_col Optional character scalar naming trial identifier column. condition_col Optional character scalar naming focal condition column. time_col Optional character scalar naming linear time trial order column. define time-course autocorrelation model. predictors Character vector naming additional predictors. interaction Optional character vector length two naming one prespecified two-way interaction. Higher-order multiple interactions supported initial contract. random_slope Logical scalar indicating whether one participant-level random slope focal condition requested. Readiness must assessed separately fitting. outcome_unit Optional character scalar recording outcome unit. required duration family must NULL binary family. notes Optional character vector containing user-supplied design analysis notes. Notes override approved model contract.

title: Create an Approved Bayesian Model Contract — create_model_contract
text: object class gp3bayes_model_contract. named list containing approved methodological specification, neutral column mappings, requested model structure, assumptions, diagnostics, sensitivity requirements, limitations, unsupported uses.

title: Create an Approved Bayesian Model Contract — create_model_contract
text: returned object specification audit record. validate data frame, construct backend formula, fit model, imply proposed analysis appropriate. gates handled separate workflows. binary contract uses Bernoulli likelihood logit link. duration contract uses lognormal likelihood strictly positive, finite, uncensored durations.

title: Create an Approved Bayesian Model Contract — create_model_contract
text: Contract creation establish causal identification, model adequacy, convergence, predictive validity, substantive validity. Behavioural measurements must interpreted direct measures latent psychological protected attributes.

title: Create an Approved Bayesian Model Contract — create_model_contract
code: binary_contract <- create_model_contract(   family = "binary",   outcome_col = "selected",   participant_col = "participant_id",   item_col = "stimulus_id",   trial_col = "trial_id",   condition_col = "condition" )  binary_contract #> <gp3bayes_model_contract> #>   Family: binary #>   Likelihood: Bernoulli #>   Link: logit #>   Outcome: selected #>   Participant: participant_id #>   Item: stimulus_id #>   Condition: condition #>   Random slope requested: FALSE #>   Fitting performed: FALSE  duration_contract <- create_model_contract(   family = "duration",   outcome_col = "response_time",   participant_col = "participant_id",   trial_col = "trial_id",   condition_col = "condition",   outcome_unit = "milliseconds" )  duration_contract #> <gp3bayes_model_contract> #>   Family: duration #>   Likelihood: lognormal #>   Link: identity on mean log duration #>   Outcome: response_time #>   Participant: participant_id #>   Condition: condition #>   Outcome unit: milliseconds #>   Random slope requested: FALSE #>   Fitting performed: FALSE

what: gp3bayes: Contract-First Bayesian Workflows for Hierarchical Behavioural Data — gp3bayes-package
title: gp3bayes: Contract-First Bayesian Workflows for Hierarchical Behavioural Data — gp3bayes-package
text: gp3bayes provides package-neutral infrastructure transparent Bayesian analysis repeated-measures hierarchical behavioural data. package emphasises explicit data model contracts, readiness auditing, deterministic simulation, inspectable prior specifications, diagnostic assessment, sensitivity analysis, conservative reporting.

title: gp3bayes: Contract-First Bayesian Workflows for Hierarchical Behavioural Data — gp3bayes-package
text: initial development scope restricted : hierarchical Bernoulli-logit models binary trial-level outcomes; hierarchical lognormal models strictly positive uncensored durations. Additional outcome families require separate methodological approval.

title: gp3bayes: Contract-First Bayesian Workflows for Hierarchical Behavioural Data — gp3bayes-package
text: Core validation, contract, simulation functionality must remain usable without Bayesian backend. Model fitting use one optional backend restricted, contract-aware interfaces rather unrestricted general-purpose formula wrapper.

title: gp3bayes 0.0.0.9000
text: Created independent gp3bayes package scaffold. Defined initial scope contract-first Bayesian workflows hierarchical behavioural data. Restricted initial development hierarchical Bernoulli-logit hierarchical lognormal-duration model families. Added package-level documentation explicit interpretation boundaries. Added initial deterministic scope test using testthat edition 3. Added MIT licence. Added standard GitHub Actions workflows cross-platform R CMD check pkgdown deployment. Added canonical repository, issue-tracker, pkgdown website metadata. Added create_model_contract() two approved initial model families neutral column mappings explicit methodological specifications. Added concise gp3bayes_model_contract print method deterministic validation tests. Added audit_model_readiness() backend-independent assessment outcome validity, declared columns, missingness, repeated measurements, item trial structure, predictors, interactions, time terms, requested participant-level random slopes. Added structured gp3bayes_readiness_audit results explicit pass, warning, failure statuses concise print method.
